1

###  Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) every _firm_ permitted to carry on the _regulated activities_ that are referred to in column (2) of the table in [6.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#12af921379f14103b61b9d22cdb4bdc8); and
  2. (2) a _CRR consolidation entity_.

*Effective: 04/11/2024*

1.1A

Where this Part requires a _data item_ to be submitted on a consolidated basis or _sub-consolidated basis_ the _CRR consolidation entity_ must comply on that basis and for this purpose, references to a _firm_ in this Part, other than in [1.1(1)](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#04244f90348f4c8fb405aefa1d91dc6c "1.1\(1\)") and [24](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#34f2089a3b704c70bceddec56e084299), mean a _CRR consolidation entity_.

*Effective: 01/01/2026*
  * [ Future  version of 1.1A after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#0d7a136ebcff49369b61162b4097deb5)

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

_administering a home finance transaction_

means any of _administering a regulated mortgage contract_ , _administering a home purchase plan_ , _administering a home reversion plan_ or _administering a regulated sale and rent back agreement_.

_administering a home purchase plan_

means the _regulated activity_ specified in article 63F(2) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_administering a home reversion plan_

means the _regulated activity_ specified in article 63B(2) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_administering a regulated mortgage contract_

means the _regulated activity_ , specified in article 61(2) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_administering a regulated sale and rent back agreement_

means the _regulated activity_ specified in article 63J(2) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_Capital+ changeover date_

means any _Capital+ reference date_ following the _initial Capital+ reference date_ applicable to a _firm_ where a _firm_ satisfies a _Capital+ condition_ which is different to the _Capital+ condition_ it satisfied on the _Capital+ reference date_ immediately preceding it.

_Capital+ condition_

means any of _Capital+ condition 1_ , _Capital+ condition 2_ , _Capital+ condition 3_ , _Capital+ condition 4_ , _Capital+ condition 5_ , _Capital+ condition 6_ , _Capital+ condition 7_ and _Capital+ condition 8_.

_Capital+ condition 1_

has the meaning given in [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6").

_Capital+ condition 2_

has the meaning given in [20.7](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#8e930d06b41446fd89be56d1ad3976e0 "20.7").

_Capital+ condition 3_

has the meaning given in [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8").

_Capital+ condition 4_

has the meaning given in [20.9](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#caddf5427bed4891acfe38e222930fa5 "20.9").

_Capital+ condition 5_

has the meaning given in [20.10](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#eda6c83544364205be2e8dbf05418c50 "20.10") and [20.10A](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#2030321f0e22403ca0e103a5ada032d7 "20.10A").

_Capital+ condition 6_

has the meaning given in [20.11](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#be81da1bb2fa4ff49a8b3391bb224401 "20.11").

_Capital+ condition 7_

has the meaning given in [20.12](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#a65e1a78447346f28e99f769a5e5013a "20.12").

_Capital+ condition 8_

has the meaning given in [20.13](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#49e5de1ba89645438ba5fffacb402836 "20.13").

_Capital+ reference date_

means:

     1. (1) a _firm’s_ _accounting reference date_ ; and
     2. (2) the date falling six months after a _firm’s_ _accounting reference date_.

_Capital+ reporting table_

has the meaning given in [20.21](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#a08591b07f90432fbe20f612a47681c8 "20.21").

_contractual reversion rate_

means the interest rate on a _regulated mortgage contract_ to which the interest rate which applies at the date of the mortgage transaction will change at the end of any initial incentive or fixed rate period.

_credit institution_

means an _undertaking_ the business of which is to take deposits or other repayable funds from the public and to grant credits for its own account, not including entities referred to in Art 2(5) of Directive 2013/36/EU.

_entering as provider into a funeral plan contract_

means the _regulated activity_ specified in article 59 of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_entering into a home finance transaction_

means any of _entering into a regulated mortgage contract_ , _entering into a home purchase plan_ , _entering into a home reversion plan_ or _entering into a regulated sale and rent back agreement_.

_entering into a home purchase plan_

means the _regulated activity_ , specified in article 63F(1) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_entering into a home reversion plan_

means the _regulated activity_ , specified in article 63B(1) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_entering into a regulated mortgage contract_

means the _regulated activity_ , specified in article 61(1) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_entering into a regulated sale and rent back agreement_

means the _regulated activity_ , specified in article 63J(1) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_establishing, operating or winding up a collective investment scheme_

means the _regulated activity_ specified in article 51ZE of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_establishing, operating or winding-up a regulated collective investment scheme _

means _establishing, operating or winding up a collective investment scheme_ if the scheme is a _regulated collective investment scheme_.

_FOS Ltd._

means the _body corporate_ established under paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 17 to FSMA (The Scheme Operator) (as originally enacted).

_further advance_

means a further loan to an existing borrower of the _firm_ and which is secured on the same property, whether under a new mortgage contract or by variation to an existing mortgage contract, including a loan secured by a second or subsequent charge where the _firm_ has the benefit of the first charge (or other charge ranking in priority to that on which the _further advance_ is secured).

_home finance administration_

means any of the _regulated activities_ of:

     1. (1) _administering a regulated mortgage contract_ ;
     2. (2) _administering a home purchase plan_ ;
     3. (3) _administering a home reversion plan_ ;
     4. (4) _administering a regulated sale and rent back agreement_ ; or
     5. (5) _agreeing to carry on a regulated activity_ in (1) to (4).

_home finance administrator_

means a _firm_ with _permission_ (or which ought to have _permission_) for _administering a home finance transaction_.

_home finance provider_

means a _firm_ with _permission_ (or which ought to have _permission_) for _entering into a home finance transaction_.

_home finance providing activity_

means any of the _regulated activities_ of:

     1. (1) _entering into a regulated mortgage contract_ ;
     2. (2) _entering into a regulated sale and rent back agreement_ ;
     3. (3) _entering into a home purchase plan_ ;
     4. (4) _entering into a home reversion plan_ ; or
     5. (5) _agreeing to carry on a regulated activity_ in (1) to (4).

_individual consolidation permission_

means a _CRR permission_ under Article 9 of the _[CRR](../#glossary-term-cbe45e8f942b45a4a7fe0d3550a6f2f3 "CRR")_.

_initial Capital+ reference date_

means:

     1. (1) in relation to a _data item_ which a _ring-fenced body_ must submit on a _sub-consolidated basis_ in accordance with [20.22A](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#1bfb6199e94e4df7b7854d3226fd8c78 "20.22A"), the first of the _firm’s_ _Capital+ reference dates_ after the _firm_ became subject to the [Ring-fenced Bodies](/pra-rules/ring-fenced-bodies " Ring-fenced Bodies") Part; or
     2. (2) for any other _firm_ , the first of the _firm’s_ _Capital+ reference dates_ after it became a _PRA-authorised person_.

_lead regulated firm_

means a _firm_ which is the subject of the financial supervision requirements of an _overseas regulator_ in accordance with an agreement between the _PRA_ and that regulator relating to the financial supervision of _firms_ whose head office is within the country of that regulator.

_meeting of repayment claims_

means the _regulated activity_ , specified in article 63N(1)(a) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_new Capital+ condition_

means, in respect of any _Capital+ changeover date_ , the new _Capital+ condition_ which a _firm_ satisfies on that date.

_non-UK bank_

means a _bank_ which is a _body corporate_ or _partnership_ formed under the law of any country or territory outside the _UK_.

_old Capital+ condition_

means, in respect of any _Capital+ changeover date_ , the _Capital+ condition_ which a _firm_ no longer satisfies on that date.

_operational continuity firm_

means a _firm_ to which the [Operational Continuity](/pra-rules/operational-continuity "Operational Continuity") Part applies.

_RAG_

means a _regulated activity group_.

_regulated activity group _

means a set of one or more _regulated activities_ referred to in determining a _firm’s_ _data item_ submission requirements.

_regulated mortgage contract_

means the investment specified in Article 61(3)(a) of the _Regulated Activities Order_.

_reporting level_

means (in relation to a _data item_) the basis on which that _data item_ is prepared, being either:

     1. (1) an individual basis; or
     2. (2) the basis of a group and, if it is prepared on the basis of a group, the type of group (such as a _domestic liquidity sub-group_).

_retail deposits_

means “deposits” within the meaning given in paragraph 30, Part 1 of Annex V to the _Supervisory Reporting ITS_ , from “households” within the meaning of paragraph 35(f) of Part 1 of Annex V to the _Supervisory Reporting ITS_.

_second charge regulated mortgage contract_

means a _regulated mortgage contract_ which is not a legal mortgage ranking in priority ahead of all other mortgages (if any) affecting the property in question, where ‘mortgage’ includes charge and (in Scotland) a heritable security.

_sole trader_

means an individual who is a _firm_.

*Effective: 31/12/2025*
  * [ Future  version of 1.2 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#3bfb34dc8f6c4799a45783a536b6df5e)

1.3

Unless otherwise defined, any italicised expression used in this Part and in the _CRR_ has the same meaning as in the _CRR_.

*Effective: 01/01/2020*

2

###  Reporting Requirements – Data Items

2.1

Unless otherwise stated, a _firm_ permitted to carry on any of the _regulated activities_ within any _RAG_ set out in column (1) of the table in [6.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#12af921379f14103b61b9d22cdb4bdc8 "6.1") must submit to the _PRA_ the _data items_ , applicable to the relevant _RAG_ as specified in the corresponding rule referred to in column (3) of that table.

*Effective: 04/11/2024*

2.2

Where, in accordance with [2.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cadc43fe4bdd453088b7098e31eb6a1f "2.1"), a _firm_ is required to submit _data items_ for more than one _RAG_ , that _firm_ must only submit the _data item_ of the same name and purpose in respect of the lowest numbered _RAG_ applicable to it, _RAG_ 1 being the lowest and _RAG_ 5 the highest.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

2.2A

Where, in accordance with [2.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cadc43fe4bdd453088b7098e31eb6a1f "2.1"), a _firm_ is required to submit the _data item_ in respect of statement of profit or loss under any _RAG_ , that _firm_ is not required to submit the _data item_ in respect of income statement under any _RAG_.

*Effective: 01/01/2018*

2.3

Where, but for [2.2](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#2fcea4d66d0d4b58ae3d6f6a9f1c8ff5 "2.2"), a _firm_ is required to submit _data items_ for more than one _RAG_ and this includes the submission of _data items_ in respect of fees, the _FOS Ltd._ or _FSCS_ levy, or _threshold conditions_ , that _firm_ must only submit these _data items_ if required to do so for the lowest numbered of the _RAGs_ applicable to the _firm_.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

2.4

Unless otherwise stated, any _data items_ to be submitted in accordance with [2.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cadc43fe4bdd453088b7098e31eb6a1f "2.1") to [2.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#fc961d3d08fa47e88f9b20fa0ab15308 "2.3") by a _non-UK bank_ should cover the activities of the _branch_ operation in the _UK_ only.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

2.5

A _firm_ that is a member of a _financial conglomerate_ must also submit _data items_ as required by [Chapter 12](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#77cd0715cea649f0b9ce4b49d1626a5d "Chapter 12").

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

2.6

[Deleted.]

*Effective: 01/01/2019*

2.7

A _bank_ must also submit _data items_ as required by [Chapter 17](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#29851eef8ef147f5a751cf680bddfa0b "Chapter 17").

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

2.8

A _CRR firm_ must also submit _data items_ as required by [Chapter 20](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4754a959e2724b0b885eaf11aab02b85 "Chapter 20").

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

2.9

[Deleted]

*Effective: 31/12/2025*

2.10

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A _third country firm_ , that is a _bank_ or _designated investment firm_ , must also submit _data items_ as required by [Chapter 22](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#6c6328cc2d5f4ec1a4da0f9eb351e9c5 "Chapter 22").

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

2.11

A _firm_ permitted to carry on the _regulated activity_ of entering into a _regulated mortgage contract_ must also submit _data items_ as required by [Chapter 23](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#45f7d9712d3248c89f12aaa9576f9c1d "Chapter 23").

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

2.12

A CRR firm (other than an SDDT) and a CRR consolidation entity (other than an SDDT consolidation entity) must also submit data items as required by [Chapter 24](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#34f2089a3b704c70bceddec56e084299).

*Effective: 01/01/2026*

3

###  Reporting Requirements – Frequency and Period

3.1

Where a _firm_ is required to submit _data items_ in accordance with [2.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cadc43fe4bdd453088b7098e31eb6a1f "Chapter 2.1") to [2.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#fc961d3d08fa47e88f9b20fa0ab15308 "2.3"), it must submit this information at the frequency and in respect of the periods specified in the rule referred to in the relevant row of column (4) of the table in [6.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#12af921379f14103b61b9d22cdb4bdc8 "6.1").

*Effective: 04/11/2024*

4

###  Reporting Requirements – Submission Dates

4.1

Where a _firm_ is required to submit _data items_ in accordance with [2.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cadc43fe4bdd453088b7098e31eb6a1f "Chapter 2.1") to [2.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#fc961d3d08fa47e88f9b20fa0ab15308 "2.3"), it must submit this information by the due date specified in the rule referred to in the relevant row of column (4) of the table in [6.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#12af921379f14103b61b9d22cdb4bdc8 "6.1").

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

4.2

If the due date for submission of a _data item_ required by this Part falls on a day which is not a _business day_ , the _data item_ must be submitted so as to be received by the _PRA_ no later than the first _business day_ after the due date.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

4.3

Unless otherwise stated, if the reporting frequency for submission of a _data item_ required by this Part is expressed as half yearly, quarterly or monthly and the due date for submission is expressed as a set period of time:

  1. (1) the first due date for submission after the _firm’s accounting reference date_ is to be calculated as the last day of that set period from the _accounting reference date_ ; and
  2. (2) thereafter, until the _firm’s_ next _accounting reference date_ , the due dates for submission are to be calculated as the last day of that set period from:
     1. (a) the date falling six months after the _firm’s_ ___accounting reference date_ , in the case of half yearly reporting frequency;
     2. (b) the dates falling every three months after the _firm’s_ ___accounting reference date_ , in the case of quarterly reporting frequency; and
     3. (c) the dates falling every month after the ___firm’s_ ___accounting reference date_ , in the case of monthly reporting frequency.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

5

###  Reporting Requirements – Submission Method

5.1

Unless otherwise stated, where a _firm_ is required to submit _data items_ in accordance with [Chapter 2](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#7a68c9aee94149418fb631df7621da5c "Chapter 2"), it must submit this information by electronic means made available by the _PRA_.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

5.2

[5.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#2f86b4c5843a49b3b249ab5edaf7192e "5.1") does not apply to:

  1. (1) [deleted.]
  2. (2) firms in _RAG_ 2.1 in relation to the reporting requirements for _RAG_ 2.1 _regulated activities_ ; and
  3. (3) those _data items_ specified as "No standard format".

*Effective: 03/01/2017*

5.3

Unless otherwise stated, _firms_ must submit _data items_ specified as “No standard format” via electronic mail to regulatoryreporting@bankofengland.co.uk or via post or hand delivery to Regulatory Data Group, Statistics and Regulatory Data Division (HO5 A-B), Bank of England, Threadneedle Street, London EC2R 8AH; or via fax to the Regulatory Data Group of the Bank of England (020 7601 3334).

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

5.4

A _data item_ must:

  1. (1) give the firm reference number (or all the firm reference numbers in those cases where a _data item_ is submitted on behalf of a number of _firms_); and
  2. (2) if submitted in paper form, be submitted with the cover sheet specified in [16.25](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#65569b918aa048f7adad05aba8967034 "16.25") duly completed.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

12.1

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

This Chapter applies only to a _firm_ that is a member of a _financial conglomerate_ and either:

  1. (1) it is at the head of a UK-regulated _financial conglomerate_ ; or

     1. (a) applies [12.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cc968e9b8f10493fb8cd99c9600b3ad3 "12.3") to the _firm_ ; or

(b) applies [12.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cc968e9b8f10493fb8cd99c9600b3ad3 "12.3") to the _firm_ unless the _UK_ _mixed financial holding company_ of the _financial conglomerate_ to which the _firm_ belongs submits the report required under this rule (as if the rule applied to it).

12.2

_Firms_ must submit to the _PRA_ the duly completed _data items_ specified in [12.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cc968e9b8f10493fb8cd99c9600b3ad3 "12.3") in accordance with [12.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#cc968e9b8f10493fb8cd99c9600b3ad3 "12.3").

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

12.3

The table below sets out the following:

  1. (a) the applicable _data items_ ;
  2. (b) the applicable reporting frequencies for submission of _data items_ and periods (calculated from a _firm's accounting reference date_, unless indicated otherwise); and
  3. (c) the applicable due dates for submission. The due dates are the last day of the periods given in the table below following the relevant reporting frequency period, unless indicated otherwise.

Financial conglomerates|  |  |
---|---|---|---
Content of Report|  _Data item_ (1)| Frequency| Due Date

Identification of significant _risk concentration_ levels | (3) | Annually | 4 months after year end
Identification of significant _intra-group transactions_ | (4) | Annually | 4 months after year end

  1. (1) When giving the report required, a _firm_ must use the form indicated, if any.
  2. (2) Adequate information must be provided, specifying the calculation method used and each _financial conglomerate_ for which the _PRA_ is the _co-ordinator_ must discuss with the _PRA_ the form which this reporting will take and the extent to which verification by an auditor will be required.
  3. (3) Rather than specifying a standard format for each _financial conglomerate_ to use, each _financial conglomerate_ for which the _PRA_ is the _co-ordinator_ must discuss with the _PRA_ the form of the information to be reported. This should mean that usual information management systems of the _financial conglomerate_ can be used to the extent possible to generate and analyse the information required.
  4. (4) For the purposes of this reporting requirement, an _intra-group transaction_ will be presumed to be significant if its amount exceeds 5% of the total amount of capital adequacy requirements at the level of the _financial conglomerate_.
Rather than specifying a standard format for each _financial conglomerate_ to use, each _financial conglomerate_ for which the PRA is the _co-ordinator_ must discuss with the PRA the form of the information to be reported.

(b) _insurance conglomerate_ : frequency is annually with due date four months after period end for the capital adequacy return and three months after period end for the report on compliance with [Financial Conglomerates 4.2](/pra-rules/financial-conglomerates#5b5f6590cfbd455ea9bfc7dccf05686d "Financial Conglomerates 4.2") where it applies.

Organogram showing each _authorised person_ in the _firm’s_ ___group_ | No standard format | Annually | 6 months after the _firm’s accounting reference date_

*Effective: 01/01/2019*

17.3

A _ring-fenced body_ must submit to the _PRA_ the following _data items_ :

  1. (1) a list of all _overseas regulators_ for each legal entity in the _ring-fenced body’s_ _group_ ; and
  2. (2) a single organogram which sets out:
     1. (a) each _authorised person_ in the _ring-fenced body’s_ _group_ ;
     2. (b) each _subsidiary_ of any _ring-fenced body_ within the _group_ that is not an _authorised person_ ; and
     3. (c) each _person_ that is not an _authorised person_ in the _ring-fenced body’s_ _sub-consolidation group_.

*Effective: 01/01/2019*

17.4

The _data items_ referred to in [17.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#db89244fc74a405c9d7322e6d01e541f "17.3") must be submitted by a _ring-fenced body_ annually within 6 months after its _accounting reference date_.

*Effective: 01/01/2019*

17.5

A single _ring-fenced body_ may submit the _data items_ referred to in [17.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#db89244fc74a405c9d7322e6d01e541f "17.3") if its _group_ contains more than one _ring-fenced body_.

*Effective: 01/01/2019*

18

###  Notifications Regarding Change of Accounting Reference Date

18.1

A _firm_ must notify the _PRA_ if it changes its _accounting reference date_ by submitting the form referred to in [Notifications 10.2](/pra-rules/notifications#acd2de2ec60149b08c260292cb64a7c7 "Notifications 10.2") (Standing Data Form).

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

18.2

When a _firm_ extends its accounting period, it must make the notification in [18.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#7904b87e4eff4ae7b86a700874d58294 "18.1") before the previous _accounting reference date_.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

18.3

When a _firm_ shortens its accounting period, it must make the notification in [18.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#7904b87e4eff4ae7b86a700874d58294 "18.1") before the new _accounting reference date_.

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

18.4

The [Notifications](/pra-rules/notifications "Notifications") Part, including [Notifications 5.3A](/pra-rules/notifications#e64102be09e14e07bde89da6229f6d06 "Notifications 5.3") and [5.5](/pra-rules/notifications#1ad2f17cd5774c83bb615a3904ccc8b9 "5.5") (Core Information Requirements), applies to any notification made under [18.1](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#7904b87e4eff4ae7b86a700874d58294 "18.1").

*Effective: 01/01/2016*

20

###  Capital+ Reports

Application and definitions

20.1

This Chapter applies to every _firm _that is a _CRR firm_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

Requirement to submit Capital+ _data items_

20.2

A _firm_ which satisfies any _Capital+ condition_ on the _initial Capital+ reference date_ applicable to it must submit to the _PRA_ on a continuing basis the _data item_ applicable to the relevant _Capital+ condition_ as set out in column (2) of the _Capital+ reporting table_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.3

Where a _firm_ is required to submit a _data item_ in accordance with this Chapter, it must submit this information:

  1. (1) at the frequency specified in column (3) of the _Capital+ reporting table_ ;
  2. (2) by the due date specified in column (4) of the _Capital+ reporting table_ ; and
  3. (3) on the basis set out in the rule specified in column (5) of the _Capital+ reporting table_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.4

The first frequency period for the purposes of [20.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#82051b008e5e4a859fabc2e7dbfcc8c8 "20.3") is:

  1. (1) where a _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 7_ or _Capital+ condition 8_ on its _initial Capital+ reference date_ , one year ending with:
     1. (a) where the _firm’s_ _initial Capital+ reference date_ is its _accounting reference date_ , its _initial Capital+ reference date_ ; or
     2. (b) where [20.4](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#31e1b3d621a34a679a7de180b58d1ca8 "20.4")(1)(a) does not apply, the _firm’s_ first _accounting reference date_ following the _firm’s_ _initial Capital+ reference date_ ; or
  2. (2) for a _firm_ satisfying any other _Capital+ condition_ on its _initial Capital+ reference date_ , the frequency period specified in column (3) of the _Capital+ reporting table_ , ending with the _initial Capital+ reference date_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.5

When submitting any required _data item_ under this Chapter, a _firm_ must use the template for that _data item_ set out in [Chapter 16](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#53063d97062e497ab533fcb723cadf60 "Chapter 16").

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

_Capital+ conditions_

20.6

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 1_ :

  1. (1) if the _firm_ is _UK parent institution_ , where it has _retail deposits_ equal to or greater than £50 billion and _total assets_ equal to or greater than £320 billion on the basis of its _consolidated situation_ ;
  2. (2) if the _firm_ is controlled by a _UK parent financial holding company_ , a _UK parent mixed financial holding company_ or a _UK parent institution a_nd the _PRA_ is responsible for supervision of that holding company or _UK parent institution _on a _consolidated basis_ , where it has _retail deposits_ equal to or greater than £50 billion and _total assets_ equal to or greater than £320 billion on the basis of the _consolidated situation_ of that _UK_ holding company or _UK parent institution_.
  3. (3) if the _firm_ is not part of a _consolidation group_ , where it has _retail deposits_ equal to or greater than £50 billion and _total assets_ equal to or greater than £320 billion on an individual basis; or
  4. (4) if the _firm _is a _ring-fenced body_ that is part of a _sub-consolidation group_ , where it has _retail deposits_ equal to or greater than £50 billion and _total assets_ equal to or greater than £320 billion on a _sub-consolidated basis_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*
  * [ Future  version of 20.6 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96)

20.7

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 2_ if it:

  1. (1) satisfies _Capital+ condition 1 _in accordance with [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(1) or [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(2); and
  2. (2) has _total assets_ equal to or greater than £50 billion on an individual basis.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*
  * [ Future  version of 20.7 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#8e930d06b41446fd89be56d1ad3976e0)

20.8

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 3_ :

  1. (1) if the _firm_ is a _UK parent institution_ , where it has _retail deposits_ equal to or greater than £50 billion and _total assets_ greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on the basis of its _consolidated situation_ ;
  2. (2) if the _firm_ is controlled by a _UK parent financial holding company_ , _UK parent mixed financial holding company_ or a _UK parent institution _and the _PRA_ is responsible for supervision of that _UK_ holding company or _UK parent institution o_n a _consolidated basis_ , where it has _retail deposits_ greater than or equal to £50 billion and _total assets_ greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on the basis of the _consolidated situation_ of that _UK_ holding company or _UK parent institution_ ;
  3. (3) if the _firm_ is not part of a _consolidation group_ , where it has _retail deposits_ greater than £50 billion and _total assets_ greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on an individual basis; or
  4. (4) if the _firm _is a _ring-fenced body_ that is part of a _sub-consolidation group_ , where it has _retail deposits_ equal to or greater than £50 billion and _total assets_ greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on a _sub-consolidated basis_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*
  * [ Future  version of 20.8 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71)

20.9

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 4_ if it:

  1. (1) satisfies _Capital+ condition 3_ in accordance with [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(1) or [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(2); and
  2. (2) has _total assets_ equal to or greater than £50 billion on an individual basis.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*
  * [ Future  version of 20.9 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#caddf5427bed4891acfe38e222930fa5)

20.10

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 5_ if it:

  1. (1) is part of a _consolidation group_ ;
  2. (2) has _total assets_ greater than £5 billion:
     1. (a) if the _firm_ is a _UK parent institution_ on the basis of its _consolidated situation_ ; or
     2. (b) if the _firm_ is controlled by a _UK parent financial holding company_ , a _UK parent mixed financial holding company_ or a _UK parent institution_ and the _PRA_ is responsible for supervision of that _UK_ holding company or _UK parent institution_ on a _consolidated basis_ , on the basis of the _consolidated situation_ of that _UK_ holding company or _UK parent institution_ ; and
  3. (3) does not satisfy _Capital+ condition 1_ on the basis of [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(1) or [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(2) or _Capital+ condition 3_ on the basis of [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(1) or [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(2).

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

20.10A

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 5_ if it is a _ring-fenced body_ that is part of a _sub-consolidation group_ and has _total assets_ greater than £5 billion on a _sub-consolidated basis_ and does not satisfy _Capital+ condition 1_ on the basis of [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(4) or _Capital+ condition 3_ on the basis of [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(4).

*Effective: 01/01/2019*

20.11

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 6_ if it has _total assets_ greater than £5 billion on an individual basis and:

  1. (1) if it is not part of a _consolidation group_ , where it does not satisfy _Capital+ condition 1_ _or_ _Capital+ condition 3_ ; or
  2. (2) if it is part of a _consolidation group_ , where it does not satisfy _Capital+ condition 2_ _or_ _Capital+ condition 4_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*
  * [ Future  version of 20.11 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#be81da1bb2fa4ff49a8b3391bb224401)

20.12

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 7_ if it is part of a _consolidation group_ and it does not satisfy _Capital+ condition 1_ , _Capital+ condition 3_ or _Capital+ condition 5_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.13

A _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 8_ :

  1. (1) where it is not part of a _consolidation group_ , if it does not satisfy _Capital+ condition 1_ , _Capital+ condition 3_ or _Capital+ condition 6_ ; or
  2. (2) where a _firm_ is part of a _consolidation group_ , if it does not satisfy _Capital+ condition 2_ , _Capital+ condition 4_ or _Capital+ condition 6_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

Moving between _Capital+ conditions_

20.14

A _firm_ must consider which _Capital+ conditions_ it satisfies on every _Capital+ reference date_ following its _initial Capital+ reference date_ , and where a _Capital+ reference date_ is a _Capital+ changeover date_ :

  1. (1) [20.16](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#de797181540947969aa5650965c4805c "20.16") applies where the _new Capital+ condition_ is higher, and
  2. (2) [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18") applies where the _new Capital+ condition_ is lower.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.15

For the purposes of [20.14](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c4766fba6ca545919c3bc4ed8b5156dc "20.14") and [20.20](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#006726bbe0a849b48daf177e14f678b6 "20.20"), _Capital+ condition 1_ is the highest and _Capital+ condition 8_ is the lowest.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.16

Where this rule applies as a result of [20.14](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c4766fba6ca545919c3bc4ed8b5156dc "20.14")(1) or [20.20](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#006726bbe0a849b48daf177e14f678b6 "20.20"), the _firm_ must:

  1. (1) submit to the _PRA_ the _data item_ applicable to the _old Capital+ condition_ in respect of the frequency period ending with that _Capital+ changeover date_ by the due date applicable to the _old Capital+ condition_ ;
  2. (2) after it has complied with 20.16(1), cease submitting the _data item_ applicable to the _old Capital+ condition_ to the _PRA_ ; and
  3. (3) submit to the _PRA_ on a continuing basis the _data item_ applicable to the _new Capital+ condition_ , as set out in column (2) of the _Capital+ reporting table_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.17

The first frequency period for the purposes of [20.16](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#de797181540947969aa5650965c4805c "20.16")(3) is the frequency period specified in column (3) of the _Capital+ reporting table_ , starting with the _Capital+ changeover date_ which caused [20.16](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#de797181540947969aa5650965c4805c "20.16") to apply.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.18

Subject to [20.20](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#006726bbe0a849b48daf177e14f678b6 "20.20"), where this rule applies as a result of [20.14](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c4766fba6ca545919c3bc4ed8b5156dc "20.14")(2), the _firm_ must:

  1. (1) continue to submit to the _PRA_ the _data item_ applicable to the _old Capital+ condition_ until the _firm’s_ next _Capital+ reference date_ , including the _data item_ due in respect of the frequency period ending with that next _Capital+ reference date_ ;
  2. (2) after it has complied with [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18")(1), cease submitting the _data item_ applicable to the _old Capital+ condition_ to the _PRA_ ; and
  3. (3) submit to the _PRA_ on a continuing basis the _data item_ applicable to the _new Capital+ condition_ , as set out in column (2) of the _Capital+ reporting table_.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

20.19

The first frequency period for the purposes of [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18")(3) is:

  1. (1) where the _data item_ required to be submitted under [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18")(3) is PRA103, one year starting from:
     1. (a) where the _Capital+ changeover date_ which caused [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18")(2) to apply is the same as the _firm’s_ _accounting reference date_ , that _Capital+ changeover date_ ; or
     2. (b) where 20.19(1)(a) does not apply, the _firm’s _first _accounting reference date_ following the _Capital+ changeover date_ which caused [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18") to apply; or
  2. (2) where the _data item_ required to be submitted under [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18")(3) is PRA101 or PRA102, the frequency period specified in column (3) of the _Capital+ reporting table_ , starting with the next _Capital+ reference date_ after the _Capital+ changeover date_ which caused [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18") to apply.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*
  * [ Future  version of 20.19 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#502006a8d58343919ae3fb6fe016929c)

20.20

Where [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18") applies as a result of [20.14](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c4766fba6ca545919c3bc4ed8b5156dc "20.14")(2), and where at any _Capital+ reference date_ before a _[firm](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "firm")_ is required to submit its first _data item_ under [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18")(3), the _[firm](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "firm")_ satisfies a higher _Capital+ condition_ than the _new Capital+ condition_ which caused [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18") to apply:

  1. (1) the changes specified in [20.18](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#4f9c5f65d8ef4608a6ce705750078133 "20.18") cease to take effect; and
  2. (2) [20.16](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#de797181540947969aa5650965c4805c "20.16") applies.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*

Basis of application

20.22

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

Where a _firm_ is required to submit a _data item_ in accordance with this rule, that _data item_ should be completed:

  1. (1) if the _firm_ is not part of a _consolidation group _and the _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 1_ on the basis of [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(3) or _Capital+ condition 3_ on the basis of [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(3), on an individual basis;
  2. (2) if the _firm_ is a _UK parent institution_ and the _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 1_ on the basis of [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(1) or _Capital+ condition 3_ on the basis of [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(1), on the basis of its _consolidated situation_ ; or
  3. (3) if the _firm_ is controlled by a _UK parent financial holding company_ , a _UK parent mixed financial holding company_ or a _UK parent institution_ and the _PRA_ is responsible for supervision of that holding company or _UK parent institution _on a _consolidated basis_ and the _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 1_ on the basis of [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(2) or _Capital+ condition 3_ on the basis of [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(2), on the basis of the _consolidated situation_ of that holding company or _UK parent institution_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*
  * [ Future  version of 20.22 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#05dc112a6ad74e2ebc8c41556681d59f)

20.22A

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

If a _firm_ meets a _Capital+ condition_ on the basis of [20.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#e464ad7f6e354dfca140dbfd07766b96 "20.6")(4), [20.8](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#3b00904374034d90970397890fb22c71 "20.8")(4) or [20.10A](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#2030321f0e22403ca0e103a5ada032d7 "20.10A"), it must submit the _data item_ on a _sub-consolidated basis_ in addition to meeting any requirement to submit a _data item_ on an individual basis or on the basis of its, its holding company’s or its _UK parent institution's_ _consolidated situation_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*
  * [ Future  version of 20.22A after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#1bfb6199e94e4df7b7854d3226fd8c78)

20.23

Where a _firm_ is required to submit a _data item_ in accordance with this rule, as set out in the _Capital+ reporting table_ , that _data item_ should be completed on an individual basis.

*Effective: 01/10/2017*
  * [ Future  version of 20.23 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#f6f6bb581233410f976be71f143fe984)

20.24

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

Where a _firm_ is required to submit a _data item_ in accordance with this rule, as set out in the _Capital+ reporting table_ , that _data item_ should be completed:

  1. (1) if the _firm_ is a _UK parent institution_ and the _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 5_ on the basis of [20.10](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#eda6c83544364205be2e8dbf05418c50 "20.10")(2)(a) or _Capital+ condition 7_ on the basis of [20.12](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#a65e1a78447346f28e99f769a5e5013a "20.12"), on the basis of its _consolidated situation_ ; or
  2. (2) if the _firm_ is controlled by a _UK parent financial holding company_ , a _UK parent mixed financial holding company_ or a _UK parent institution_ and the _PRA_ is responsible for supervision of that holding company or _UK parent institution_ on a _consolidated basis_ and the _firm_ satisfies _Capital+ condition 5_ on the basis of [20.10](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#eda6c83544364205be2e8dbf05418c50 "20.10")(2)(b) or _Capital+ condition 7_ on the basis of [20.12](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#a65e1a78447346f28e99f769a5e5013a "20.12"), on the basis of the _consolidated situation_ of that holding company or _UK parent institution_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

21

###  Operational Continuity Reporting [Deleted]

21.1

[Deleted]

*Effective: 31/12/2025*

21.2

[Deleted]

*Effective: 31/12/2025*

22

###  Branch Reporting

22.1

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

This Chapter applies only to:

  1. (1) [Deleted.]
  2. (2) a _third country firm_
  3. that is:
  4. (3) a _bank_ ; or
  5. (4) a _designated investment firm_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

22.2

A _firm_ must provide the _PRA_ with information in accordance with the Branch Return Form. Unless otherwise stated in the Branch Return Form, the information must be provided as at 30 June and 31 December each year and provided within 30 _business days_ of 30 June and 31 December, as applicable, each year.

*Effective: 01/03/2026*

22.3

The Branch Return Form can be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/regulatory-reporting/banking/2025/branch-return-template-may-2025.pdf).

*Effective: 01/03/2026*

22.4

A _third country firm_ that is a _bank_ must report the following information to the _PRA_ :

  1. (1) [deleted];
  2. (2) the _own funds_ that are at the disposal of the _branch_ ;
  3. (3) the deposit protection arrangements available to depositors in the _branch_ ;
  4. (4) the risk management arrangements;
  5. (5) the governance arrangements, including _key function holders_ for the activities of the _branch_ ; and
  6. (6) the recovery plans covering the _branch_.

[**Note** : Article 47 (1a) of the  _CRD_]

*Effective: 01/03/2026*

22.5

A _firm_ must report the information set out in [22.4](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#d6d504ee5a99427b9cf7c972d0ebbeba "22.4") annually. The information must be reported within 7 months from the end of the _firm’s_ financial year.

[Note: Article 47 (1a) of the _CRD_]

*Effective: 29/12/2020*

22.6

A _firm_ must submit the information set out in [22.4](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#d6d504ee5a99427b9cf7c972d0ebbeba "22.4") by email to their usual supervisory contact at the _PRA_. If the _firm_ has already submitted the information in the context of another data request in relation to the _firm’s_ financial year, the _firm_ can refer to that submission instead.

*Effective: 29/12/2020*

23

###  Regulatory Mortgage Contract Reporting

Application

23.1

This Chapter applies to every _firm_ permitted to carry on the _regulated activity_ of _entering into a regulated mortgage contract_.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

Reporting Requirement

23.2

If a _firm_ makes a _further advance_ under a _regulated mortgage contract_ , the _firm_ must submit to the _FCA_ a ‘sales data report’ within the meaning of SUP 16.11 of the _FCA Handbook_ which contains the _data elements_ set out in SUP 16 Annex 21 of the _FCA Handbook_.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

23.3

If a _firm_ makes an advance under a _regulated mortgage contract_ which is not a _second charge regulated mortgage contract_ , the _firm_ must submit the _data elements_ specified in [23.6](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c0a6e91a26224752a8263390556949c9 "23.6") to the _FCA_ as part of a ‘sales data report’ under SUP 16.11 of the _FCA Handbook_.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

23.4

If a _firm_ makes an advance under a _second charge regulated mortgage contract_ the _firm_ must submit the _data elements_ specified in [23.7](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#45d47e80a8334f06bd8613adc9c8e68b "23.7") to the _FCA_ as part of a ‘sales data report’ under SUP 16.11 of the _FCA Handbook_.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

23.5

  1. (1) A _firm_ may appoint another person to submit the ‘sales data report’ under [23.2](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#f01d65bd3002463cb9ef79adc36b0b9f "22.2") or the _data elements_ under [23.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#17dd71d2409f4ed4b14c4bdba0ffbac7 "23.3") or [23.4](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c637efee45b641a48f1d161e15364b4f "23.4") on the _firm’s_ behalf if the _firm_ has informed the _PRA _and the _FCA_ of that appointment in writing.
  2. (2) Where (1) applies, the _firm_ must ensure that the submission complies with the requirements of this Chapter and identifies the originator of the transaction.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

Data Elements

23.6

The _data elements_ referred to in [23.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#17dd71d2409f4ed4b14c4bdba0ffbac7 "23.3") are:

  1. (1) the total amount owed by the borrower to the _firm_ and secured on the same property immediately prior to the new borrowing– this _data element_ is not limited to amounts secured by _regulated mortgage contracts_ , but includes any borrowing secured on the property;
  2. (2) if a stress test was undertaken, the interest rate to which stress was applied in order to produce a stress-tested interest rate; and
  3. (3) if a stress test was undertaken and the _contractual reversion rate_ is different from the rate reported in (2), the _contractual reversion rate_ specified in the _regulated mortgage contract_.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

23.7

The _data elements_ referred to in [23.4](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c637efee45b641a48f1d161e15364b4f "22.4") are:

  1. (1) the total amount owed by the borrower to the _firm_ and secured on the same property immediately prior to the new borrowing– this _data element_ is not limited to amounts secured by _regulated mortgage contracts_ , but includes any borrowing secured on the property;
  2. (2) the total amount owed by the borrower to third parties secured on the property;
  3. (3) if a stress test was undertaken, the interest rate to which stress was applied in order to produce a stress-tested interest rate; and
  4. (4) if a stress test was undertaken and the _contractual reversion rate_ is different from the rate reported in (3), the _contractual reversion rate_ specified in the _second charge regulated mortgage contract_.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

Mode of Reporting

23.8

A _firm_ must submit the ’sales data report’ required by [23.2](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#f01d65bd3002463cb9ef79adc36b0b9f "22.2") and the _data elements_ required by [23.3](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#17dd71d2409f4ed4b14c4bdba0ffbac7 "23.3") and [23.4](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#c637efee45b641a48f1d161e15364b4f "23.4") in accordance with the procedure and format for a ‘sales data report’ set out in SUP 16.11 of the _FCA Handbook_.

*Effective: 01/04/2021*

24

###  Step-in Risk Reporting

24.1

This Chapter applies only to:

  1. (1) a firm that is a CRR firm but not an SDDT; and
  2. (2) a CRR consolidation entity that is not an SDDT consolidation entity.

*Effective: 01/01/2026*

24.2

A firm that is required to comply with the [Step-in Risk Part](/pra-rules/step-in-risk) on an individual basis or sub-consolidated basis must comply with this Chapter on the same basis.

*Effective: 01/01/2026*
  * [ Future  version of 24.2 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#2be41ad04f914496809f860ba1463ca4)

24.3

A CRR consolidation entity must comply with this Chapter on the same basis as it is required to comply with the [Step-in Risk Part](/pra-rules/step-in-risk) and, for this purpose, references to a firm in this Chapter (other than in [24.1(1)](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#99bef08c757e41a8b596235de2453095) and [24.2](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#2be41ad04f914496809f860ba1463ca4)) mean a CRR consolidation entity.

*Effective: 01/01/2026*
  * [ Future  version of 24.3 after 01/01/2027  ](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting/01-01-2027#b47ad049ee9248f8b7ffc03bb56d4c16)

24.4

A firm must complete:

  1. (1) data item SI700.00;
  2. (2) data item SI01.00 if the firm identifies a step-in entity; and
  3. (3) data item SI02.00 if the firm identifies a material step-in entity,

in each case for the step-in risk assessment required in the [Step-in Risk Part](/pra-rules/step-in-risk).

*Effective: 01/01/2026*

24.5

A firm must submit the data items it is required to complete by this Chapter to the PRA at the same time as the firm submits its ICAAP assessment to the PRA.

*Effective: 01/01/2026*

24.6

When submitting the required data items, a firm must use the template for the data item set out in [24.7](/pra-rules/regulatory-reporting#fb6480bc5c02443bbe40edba6e4918a0).

*Effective: 01/01/2026*

24.7

  1. (1) SI700.00 can be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/policy-statement/2025/april/templates-for-reporting-the-step-in-risk-assessment.xlsx).
  2. (2) SI01.00 can be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/policy-statement/2025/april/templates-for-reporting-the-step-in-risk-assessment.xlsx).
  3. (3) SI02.00 can be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/policy-statement/2025/april/templates-for-reporting-the-step-in-risk-assessment.xlsx).

*Effective: 01/01/2026*

1

Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) every firm permitted to carry on the regulated activities that are referred to in column (2) of the table in 6.1; and
  2. (2) a CRR consolidation entity.
  • 04/11/2024
  • Past version of 1.1 before 04/11/2024

1.1A

Where this Part requires a data item to be submitted on a consolidated basis or sub-consolidated basis the CRR consolidation entity must comply on that basis and for this purpose, references to a firm in this Part, other than in 1.1(1) and 24, mean a CRR consolidation entity.

  • 01/01/2026
  • Past version of 1.1A before 01/01/2026
  • Future version of 1.1A after 01/01/2027

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

administering a home finance transaction

means any of administering a regulated mortgage contract, administering a home purchase plan, administering a home reversion plan or administering a regulated sale and rent back agreement.

administering a home purchase plan

means the regulated activity specified in article 63F(2) of the Regulated Activities Order.

administering a home reversion plan

means the regulated activity specified in article 63B(2) of the Regulated Activities Order.

administering a regulated mortgage contract

means the regulated activity, specified in article 61(2) of the Regulated Activities Order.

administering a regulated sale and rent back agreement

means the regulated activity specified in article 63J(2) of the Regulated Activities Order.

Capital+ changeover date

means any Capital+ reference date following the initial Capital+ reference date applicable to a firm where a firm satisfies a Capital+ condition which is different to the Capital+ condition it satisfied on the Capital+ reference date immediately preceding it.

Capital+ condition

means any of Capital+ condition 1, Capital+ condition 2, Capital+ condition 3, Capital+ condition 4, Capital+ condition 5, Capital+ condition 6, Capital+ condition 7 and Capital+ condition 8.

Capital+ condition 1

has the meaning given in 20.6.

Capital+ condition 2

has the meaning given in 20.7.

Capital+ condition 3

has the meaning given in 20.8.

Capital+ condition 4

has the meaning given in 20.9.

Capital+ condition 5

has the meaning given in 20.10 and 20.10A.

Capital+ condition 6

has the meaning given in 20.11.

Capital+ condition 7

has the meaning given in 20.12.

Capital+ condition 8

has the meaning given in 20.13.

Capital+ reference date

means:

    1. (1) a firm’s accounting reference date; and
    2. (2) the date falling six months after a firm’s accounting reference date.

Capital+ reporting table

has the meaning given in 20.21.

contractual reversion rate

means the interest rate on a regulated mortgage contract to which the interest rate which applies at the date of the mortgage transaction will change at the end of any initial incentive or fixed rate period.

credit institution

means an undertaking the business of which is to take deposits or other repayable funds from the public and to grant credits for its own account, not including entities referred to in Art 2(5) of Directive 2013/36/EU.

entering as provider into a funeral plan contract

means the regulated activity specified in article 59 of the Regulated Activities Order.

entering into a home finance transaction

means any of entering into a regulated mortgage contract, entering into a home purchase plan, entering into a home reversion plan or entering into a regulated sale and rent back agreement.

entering into a home purchase plan

means the regulated activity, specified in article 63F(1) of the Regulated Activities Order.

entering into a home reversion plan

means the regulated activity, specified in article 63B(1) of the Regulated Activities Order.

entering into a regulated mortgage contract

means the regulated activity, specified in article 61(1) of the Regulated Activities Order.

entering into a regulated sale and rent back agreement

means the regulated activity, specified in article 63J(1) of the Regulated Activities Order.

establishing, operating or winding up a collective investment scheme

means the regulated activity specified in article 51ZE of the Regulated Activities Order.

establishing, operating or winding-up a regulated collective investment scheme

means establishing, operating or winding up a collective investment scheme if the scheme is a regulated collective investment scheme.

FOS Ltd.

means the body corporate established under paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 17 to FSMA (The Scheme Operator) (as originally enacted).

further advance

means a further loan to an existing borrower of the firm and which is secured on the same property, whether under a new mortgage contract or by variation to an existing mortgage contract, including a loan secured by a second or subsequent charge where the firm has the benefit of the first charge (or other charge ranking in priority to that on which the further advance is secured).

home finance administration

means any of the regulated activities of:

    1. (1) administering a regulated mortgage contract;
    2. (2) administering a home purchase plan;
    3. (3) administering a home reversion plan;
    4. (4) administering a regulated sale and rent back agreement; or
    5. (5) agreeing to carry on a regulated activity in (1) to (4).

home finance administrator

means a firm with permission (or which ought to have permission) for administering a home finance transaction.

home finance provider

means a firm with permission (or which ought to have permission) for entering into a home finance transaction.

home finance providing activity

means any of the regulated activities of:

    1. (1) entering into a regulated mortgage contract;
    2. (2) entering into a regulated sale and rent back agreement;
    3. (3) entering into a home purchase plan;
    4. (4) entering into a home reversion plan; or
    5. (5) agreeing to carry on a regulated activity in (1) to (4).

individual consolidation permission

means a CRR permission under Article 9 of the CRR.

initial Capital+ reference date

means:

    1. (1) in relation to a data item which a ring-fenced body must submit on a sub-consolidated basis in accordance with 20.22A, the first of the firm’s Capital+ reference dates after the firm became subject to the Ring-fenced Bodies Part; or
    2. (2) for any other firm, the first of the firm’s Capital+ reference dates after it became a PRA-authorised person.

lead regulated firm

means a firm which is the subject of the financial supervision requirements of an overseas regulator in accordance with an agreement between the PRA and that regulator relating to the financial supervision of firms whose head office is within the country of that regulator.

meeting of repayment claims

means the regulated activity, specified in article 63N(1)(a) of the Regulated Activities Order.

new Capital+ condition

means, in respect of any Capital+ changeover date, the new Capital+ condition which a firm satisfies on that date.

non-UK bank

means a bank which is a body corporate or partnership formed under the law of any country or territory outside the UK.

old Capital+ condition

means, in respect of any Capital+ changeover date, the Capital+ condition which a firm no longer satisfies on that date.

operational continuity firm

means a firm to which the Operational Continuity Part applies.

RAG

means a regulated activity group.

regulated activity group

means a set of one or more regulated activities referred to in determining a firm’s data item submission requirements.

regulated mortgage contract

means the investment specified in Article 61(3)(a) of the Regulated Activities Order.

reporting level

means (in relation to a data item) the basis on which that data item is prepared, being either:

    1. (1) an individual basis; or
    2. (2) the basis of a group and, if it is prepared on the basis of a group, the type of group (such as a domestic liquidity sub-group).

retail deposits

means “deposits” within the meaning given in paragraph 30, Part 1 of Annex V to the Supervisory Reporting ITS, from “households” within the meaning of paragraph 35(f) of Part 1 of Annex V to the Supervisory Reporting ITS.

second charge regulated mortgage contract

means a regulated mortgage contract which is not a legal mortgage ranking in priority ahead of all other mortgages (if any) affecting the property in question, where ‘mortgage’ includes charge and (in Scotland) a heritable security.

sole trader

means an individual who is a firm.

  • 31/12/2025
  • Past version of 1.2 before 31/12/2025
  • Future version of 1.2 after 01/01/2027

1.3

Unless otherwise defined, any italicised expression used in this Part and in the CRR has the same meaning as in the CRR.

  • 01/01/2020
  • Past version of 1.3 before 01/01/2020

2

Reporting Requirements – Data Items

2.1

Unless otherwise stated, a firm permitted to carry on any of the regulated activities within any RAG set out in column (1) of the table in 6.1 must submit to the PRA the data items, applicable to the relevant RAG as specified in the corresponding rule referred to in column (3) of that table.

  • 04/11/2024
  • Past version of 2.1 before 04/11/2024

2.2

Where, in accordance with 2.1, a firm is required to submit data items for more than one RAG, that firm must only submit the data item of the same name and purpose in respect of the lowest numbered RAG applicable to it, RAG 1 being the lowest and RAG 5 the highest.

  • 01/01/2016

2.2A

Where, in accordance with 2.1, a firm is required to submit the data item in respect of statement of profit or loss under any RAG, that firm is not required to submit the data item in respect of income statement under any RAG.

  • 01/01/2018

2.3

Where, but for 2.2, a firm is required to submit data items for more than one RAG and this includes the submission of data items in respect of fees, the FOS Ltd. or FSCS levy, or threshold conditions, that firm must only submit these data items if required to do so for the lowest numbered of the RAGs applicable to the firm.

  • 01/01/2016

2.4

Unless otherwise stated, any data items to be submitted in accordance with 2.1 to 2.3 by a non-UK bank should cover the activities of the branch operation in the UK only.

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 2.4 before 31/12/2020

2.5

A firm that is a member of a financial conglomerate must also submit data items as required by Chapter 12.

  • 01/01/2016

2.6

[Deleted.]

  • 01/01/2019
  • Past version of 2.6 before 01/01/2019

2.7

A bank must also submit data items as required by Chapter 17.

  • 01/01/2016

2.8

A CRR firm must also submit data items as required by Chapter 20.

  • 01/10/2017

2.9

[Deleted]

  • 31/12/2025
  • Past version of 2.9 before 31/12/2025

2.10

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A third country firm, that is a bank or designated investment firm, must also submit data items as required by Chapter 22.

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 2.10 before 31/12/2020

2.11

A firm permitted to carry on the regulated activity of entering into a regulated mortgage contract must also submit data items as required by Chapter 23.

  • 01/04/2021

2.12

A CRR firm (other than an SDDT) and a CRR consolidation entity (other than an SDDT consolidation entity) must also submit data items as required by Chapter 24.

  • 01/01/2026

3

Reporting Requirements – Frequency and Period

3.1

Where a firm is required to submit data items in accordance with 2.1 to 2.3, it must submit this information at the frequency and in respect of the periods specified in the rule referred to in the relevant row of column (4) of the table in 6.1.

  • 04/11/2024
  • Past version of 3.1 before 04/11/2024

4

Reporting Requirements – Submission Dates

4.1

Where a firm is required to submit data items in accordance with 2.1 to 2.3, it must submit this information by the due date specified in the rule referred to in the relevant row of column (4) of the table in 6.1.

  • 01/01/2016

4.2

If the due date for submission of a data item required by this Part falls on a day which is not a business day, the data item must be submitted so as to be received by the PRA no later than the first business day after the due date.

  • 01/01/2016

4.3

Unless otherwise stated, if the reporting frequency for submission of a data item required by this Part is expressed as half yearly, quarterly or monthly and the due date for submission is expressed as a set period of time:

  1. (1) the first due date for submission after the firm’s accounting reference date is to be calculated as the last day of that set period from the accounting reference date; and
  2. (2) thereafter, until the firm’s next accounting reference date, the due dates for submission are to be calculated as the last day of that set period from:
    1. (a) the date falling six months after the firm’s accounting reference date, in the case of half yearly reporting frequency;
    2. (b) the dates falling every three months after the firm’s accounting reference date, in the case of quarterly reporting frequency; and
    3. (c) the dates falling every month after the firm’s accounting reference date, in the case of monthly reporting frequency.
  • 01/01/2016

5

Reporting Requirements – Submission Method

5.1

Unless otherwise stated, where a firm is required to submit data items in accordance with Chapter 2, it must submit this information by electronic means made available by the PRA.

  • 01/01/2016

5.2

5.1 does not apply to:

  1. (1) [deleted.]
  2. (2) firms in RAG 2.1 in relation to the reporting requirements for RAG 2.1 regulated activities; and
  3. (3) those data items specified as "No standard format".
  • 03/01/2017
  • Past version of 5.2 before 03/01/2017

5.3

Unless otherwise stated, firms must submit data items specified as “No standard format” via electronic mail to regulatoryreporting@bankofengland.co.uk or via post or hand delivery to Regulatory Data Group, Statistics and Regulatory Data Division (HO5 A-B), Bank of England, Threadneedle Street, London EC2R 8AH; or via fax to the Regulatory Data Group of the Bank of England (020 7601 3334).

  • 01/01/2016

5.4

A data item must:

  1. (1) give the firm reference number (or all the firm reference numbers in those cases where a data item is submitted on behalf of a number of firms); and
  2. (2) if submitted in paper form, be submitted with the cover sheet specified in 16.25 duly completed.
  • 01/01/2016

6

Regulated Activity Groups

6.1

Unless otherwise indicated, firms must comply with the rules specified in the following table (which set out the data items, frequency and submission periods as applicable to each RAG) in accordance with Chapters 2, 3 and 4.

(1) (2) (3) (4)
RAG number Regulated Activities Rules containing:
applicable data items reporting frequency / period due date
RAG 1 • accepting deposits
• meeting of repayment claims
• [deleted] 		7.1, except that the requirement to:

Capital+ conditions

20.6

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 1:

  1. (1) if the firm is UK parent institution, where it has retail deposits equal to or greater than £50 billion and total assets equal to or greater than £320 billion on the basis of its consolidated situation;
  2. (2) if the firm is controlled by a UK parent financial holding company, a UK parent mixed financial holding company or a UK parent institution and the PRA is responsible for supervision of that holding company or UK parent institution on a consolidated basis, where it has retail deposits equal to or greater than £50 billion and total assets equal to or greater than £320 billion on the basis of the consolidated situation of that UK holding company or UK parent institution.
  3. (3) if the firm is not part of a consolidation group, where it has retail deposits equal to or greater than £50 billion and total assets equal to or greater than £320 billion on an individual basis; or
  4. (4) if the firm is a ring-fenced body that is part of a sub-consolidation group, where it has retail deposits equal to or greater than £50 billion and total assets equal to or greater than £320 billion on a sub-consolidated basis.
  • 31/12/2020

  • Future version of 20.6 after 01/01/2027

20.7

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 2 if it:

  1. (1) satisfies Capital+ condition 1 in accordance with 20.6(1) or 20.6(2); and
  2. (2) has total assets equal to or greater than £50 billion on an individual basis.
  • 01/10/2017
  • Future version of 20.7 after 01/01/2027

20.8

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 3:

  1. (1) if the firm is a UK parent institution, where it has retail deposits equal to or greater than £50 billion and total assets greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on the basis of its consolidated situation;
  2. (2) if the firm is controlled by a UK parent financial holding company, UK parent mixed financial holding company or a UK parent institution and the PRA is responsible for supervision of that UK holding company or UK parent institution on a consolidated basis, where it has retail deposits greater than or equal to £50 billion and total assets greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on the basis of the consolidated situation of that UK holding company or UK parent institution;
  3. (3) if the firm is not part of a consolidation group, where it has retail deposits greater than £50 billion and total assets greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on an individual basis; or
  4. (4) if the firm is a ring-fenced body that is part of a sub-consolidation group, where it has retail deposits equal to or greater than £50 billion and total assets greater than £5 billion but less than £320 billion on a sub-consolidated basis.
  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 20.8 before 31/12/2020
  • Future version of 20.8 after 01/01/2027

20.9

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 4 if it:

  1. (1) satisfies Capital+ condition 3 in accordance with 20.8(1) or 20.8(2); and
  2. (2) has total assets equal to or greater than £50 billion on an individual basis.
  • 01/10/2017
  • Future version of 20.9 after 01/01/2027

20.10

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 5 if it:

  1. (1) is part of a consolidation group;
  2. (2) has total assets greater than £5 billion:
    1. (a) if the firm is a UK parent institution on the basis of its consolidated situation; or
    2. (b) if the firm is controlled by a UK parent financial holding company, a UK parent mixed financial holding company or a UK parent institution and the PRA is responsible for supervision of that UK holding company or UK parent institution on a consolidated basis, on the basis of the consolidated situation of that UK holding company or UK parent institution; and
  3. (3) does not satisfy Capital+ condition 1 on the basis of 20.6(1) or 20.6(2) or Capital+ condition 3 on the basis of 20.8(1) or 20.8(2).
  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 20.10 before 31/12/2020

20.10A

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 5 if it is a ring-fenced body that is part of a sub-consolidation group and has total assets greater than £5 billion on a sub-consolidated basis and does not satisfy Capital+ condition 1 on the basis of 20.6(4) or Capital+ condition 3 on the basis of 20.8(4).

  • 01/01/2019

20.11

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 6 if it has total assets greater than £5 billion on an individual basis and:

  1. (1) if it is not part of a consolidation group, where it does not satisfy Capital+ condition 1 or Capital+ condition 3; or
  2. (2) if it is part of a consolidation group, where it does not satisfy Capital+ condition 2 or Capital+ condition 4.
  • 01/10/2017
  • Future version of 20.11 after 01/01/2027

20.12

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 7 if it is part of a consolidation group and it does not satisfy Capital+ condition 1, Capital+ condition 3 or Capital+ condition 5.

  • 01/10/2017

20.13

A firm satisfies Capital+ condition 8:

  1. (1) where it is not part of a consolidation group, if it does not satisfy Capital+ condition 1, Capital+ condition 3 or Capital+ condition 6; or
  2. (2) where a firm is part of a consolidation group, if it does not satisfy Capital+ condition 2, Capital+ condition 4 or Capital+ condition 6.
  • 01/10/2017

Moving between Capital+ conditions

20.14

A firm must consider which Capital+ conditions it satisfies on every Capital+ reference date following its initial Capital+ reference date, and where a Capital+ reference date is a Capital+ changeover date:

  1. (1) 20.16 applies where the new Capital+ condition is higher, and
  2. (2) 20.18 applies where the new Capital+ condition is lower.
  • 01/10/2017

20.15

For the purposes of 20.14 and 20.20, Capital+ condition 1 is the highest and Capital+ condition 8 is the lowest.

  • 01/10/2017

20.16

Where this rule applies as a result of 20.14(1) or 20.20, the firm must:

  1. (1) submit to the PRA the data item applicable to the old Capital+ condition in respect of the frequency period ending with that Capital+ changeover date by the due date applicable to the old Capital+ condition;
  2. (2) after it has complied with 20.16(1), cease submitting the data item applicable to the old Capital+ condition to the PRA; and
  3. (3) submit to the PRA on a continuing basis the data item applicable to the new Capital+ condition, as set out in column (2) of the Capital+ reporting table.
  • 01/10/2017

20.17

The first frequency period for the purposes of 20.16(3) is the frequency period specified in column (3) of the Capital+ reporting table, starting with the Capital+ changeover date which caused 20.16 to apply.

  • 01/10/2017

20.18

Subject to 20.20, where this rule applies as a result of 20.14(2), the firm must:

  1. (1) continue to submit to the PRA the data item applicable to the old Capital+ condition until the firm’s next Capital+ reference date, including the data item due in respect of the frequency period ending with that next Capital+ reference date;
  2. (2) after it has complied with 20.18(1), cease submitting the data item applicable to the old Capital+ condition to the PRA; and
  3. (3) submit to the PRA on a continuing basis the data item applicable to the new Capital+ condition, as set out in column (2) of the Capital+ reporting table.
  • 01/10/2017

20.19

The first frequency period for the purposes of 20.18(3) is:

  1. (1) where the data item required to be submitted under 20.18(3) is PRA103, one year starting from:
    1. (a) where the Capital+ changeover date which caused 20.18(2) to apply is the same as the firm’s accounting reference date, that Capital+ changeover date; or
    2. (b) where 20.19(1)(a) does not apply, the firm’s first accounting reference date following the Capital+ changeover date which caused 20.18 to apply; or
  2. (2) where the data item required to be submitted under 20.18(3) is PRA101 or PRA102, the frequency period specified in column (3) of the Capital+ reporting table, starting with the next Capital+ reference date after the Capital+ changeover date which caused 20.18 to apply.
  • 01/10/2017
  • Future version of 20.19 after 01/01/2027

20.20

Where 20.18 applies as a result of 20.14(2), and where at any Capital+ reference date before a firm is required to submit its first data item under 20.18(3), the firm satisfies a higher Capital+ condition than the new Capital+ condition which caused 20.18 to apply:

  1. (1) the changes specified in 20.18 cease to take effect; and
  2. (2) 20.16 applies.
  • 01/10/2017

Basis of application

20.22

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

Where a firm is required to submit a data item in accordance with this rule, that data item should be completed:

  1. (1) if the firm is not part of a consolidation group and the firm satisfies Capital+ condition 1 on the basis of 20.6(3) or Capital+ condition 3 on the basis of 20.8(3), on an individual basis;
  2. (2) if the firm is a UK parent institution and the firm satisfies Capital+ condition 1 on the basis of 20.6(1) or Capital+ condition 3 on the basis of 20.8(1), on the basis of its consolidated situation; or
  3. (3) if the firm is controlled by a UK parent financial holding company, a UK parent mixed financial holding company or a UK parent institution and the PRA is responsible for supervision of that holding company or UK parent institution on a consolidated basis and the firm satisfies Capital+ condition 1 on the basis of 20.6(2) or Capital+ condition 3 on the basis of 20.8(2), on the basis of the consolidated situation of that holding company or UK parent institution.
  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 20.22 before 31/12/2020
  • Future version of 20.22 after 01/01/2027

20.22A

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

If a firm meets a Capital+ condition on the basis of 20.6(4), 20.8(4) or 20.10A, it must submit the data item on a sub-consolidated basis in addition to meeting any requirement to submit a data item on an individual basis or on the basis of its, its holding company’s or its UK parent institution's consolidated situation.

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 20.22A before 31/12/2020
  • Future version of 20.22A after 01/01/2027

20.23

Where a firm is required to submit a data item in accordance with this rule, as set out in the Capital+ reporting table, that data item should be completed on an individual basis.

  • 01/10/2017
  • Future version of 20.23 after 01/01/2027

20.24

The changes to this rule are effective from 23:00 on 31/12/2020.

Where a firm is required to submit a data item in accordance with this rule, as set out in the Capital+ reporting table, that data item should be completed:

  1. (1) if the firm is a UK parent institution and the firm satisfies Capital+ condition 5 on the basis of 20.10(2)(a) or Capital+ condition 7 on the basis of 20.12, on the basis of its consolidated situation; or
  2. (2) if the firm is controlled by a UK parent financial holding company, a UK parent mixed financial holding company or a UK parent institution and the PRA is responsible for supervision of that holding company or UK parent institution on a consolidated basis and the firm satisfies Capital+ condition 5 on the basis of 20.10(2)(b) or Capital+ condition 7 on the basis of 20.12, on the basis of the consolidated situation of that holding company or UK parent institution.
  • 31/12/2020

23

Regulatory Mortgage Contract Reporting

Application

23.1

This Chapter applies to every firm permitted to carry on the regulated activity of entering into a regulated mortgage contract.

  • 01/04/2021

Reporting Requirement

23.2

If a firm makes a further advance under a regulated mortgage contract, the firm must submit to the FCA a ‘sales data report’ within the meaning of SUP 16.11 of the FCA Handbook which contains the data elements set out in SUP 16 Annex 21 of the FCA Handbook.

  • 01/04/2021

23.3

If a firm makes an advance under a regulated mortgage contract which is not a second charge regulated mortgage contract, the firm must submit the data elements specified in 23.6 to the FCA as part of a ‘sales data report’ under SUP 16.11 of the FCA Handbook.

  • 01/04/2021

23.4

If a firm makes an advance under a second charge regulated mortgage contract the firm must submit the data elements specified in 23.7 to the FCA as part of a ‘sales data report’ under SUP 16.11 of the FCA Handbook.

  • 01/04/2021

23.5

  1. (1) A firm may appoint another person to submit the ‘sales data report’ under 23.2 or the data elements under 23.3 or 23.4 on the firm’s behalf if the firm has informed the PRA and the FCA of that appointment in writing.
  2. (2) Where (1) applies, the firm must ensure that the submission complies with the requirements of this Chapter and identifies the originator of the transaction.
  • 01/04/2021

Data Elements

23.6

The data elements referred to in 23.3 are:

  1. (1) the total amount owed by the borrower to the firm and secured on the same property immediately prior to the new borrowing– this data element is not limited to amounts secured by regulated mortgage contracts, but includes any borrowing secured on the property;
  2. (2) if a stress test was undertaken, the interest rate to which stress was applied in order to produce a stress-tested interest rate; and
  3. (3) if a stress test was undertaken and the contractual reversion rate is different from the rate reported in (2), the contractual reversion rate specified in the regulated mortgage contract.
  • 01/04/2021

23.7

The data elements referred to in 23.4 are:

  1. (1) the total amount owed by the borrower to the firm and secured on the same property immediately prior to the new borrowing– this data element is not limited to amounts secured by regulated mortgage contracts, but includes any borrowing secured on the property;
  2. (2) the total amount owed by the borrower to third parties secured on the property;
  3. (3) if a stress test was undertaken, the interest rate to which stress was applied in order to produce a stress-tested interest rate; and
  4. (4) if a stress test was undertaken and the contractual reversion rate is different from the rate reported in (3), the contractual reversion rate specified in the second charge regulated mortgage contract.
  • 01/04/2021

Mode of Reporting

23.8

A firm must submit the ’sales data report’ required by 23.2 and the data elements required by 23.3 and 23.4 in accordance with the procedure and format for a ‘sales data report’ set out in SUP 16.11 of the FCA Handbook.

  • 01/04/2021
